title: Aster chekiangensis
taxonomy: Aster chekiangensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Aster and is native to China. The classification of Aster chekiangensis is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, clade - Angiosperms, clade - Eudicots, clade - Asterids, order - Asterales, family - Asteraceae, genus - Aster, species - A. chekiangensis. This classification groups Aster chekiangensis among other similar flowering plants. The family Asteraceae is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants.
uses: Aster chekiangensis is a plant species that has been used in traditional Chinese medicine. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant is often used to treat various ailments such as fever, sore throat, and respiratory issues. Its medicinal uses include treating digestive problems and skin conditions. Additionally, it may help reduce stress and anxiety due to its calming effects.

parts: Aster chekiangensis, also known as Chekiang Aster, is a plant species native to China. The medicinal parts of Aster chekiangensis include its flowers, roots, and leaves, which have been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions.
